chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI HCA No. 375 of 2023 _____________________________________________________________ ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ______________________________________________________________ Disposed of 1. For order on CMA No.2733/2024(U/A) 2. For order on CMA No.2734/2024(U/A) 28.10.2024 M/s. Salahuddin Ahmed and Nadeem Ahmed, advocate for appellant M/s. Abid S. Zuberi, Ayan Mustafa Memon and Muhammad Arif Ansari, advocate for respondent No.1 Mr. Khawaja Shamsul Islam, advocate for respondent No.2 M/s. Omer Soomro and Danish Nayar, advocate for respondent No.4 ------ 1. Granted. 2. Suit 1169 of 2013 was filed in the year 2013 and CMA 9983 of 2013 was filed therein seeking interim relief. Ad interim orders operated in respect thereof till the application was dismissed vide order dated 06.10.2023. The present appeal was preferred on 24.10.2023 and ad interim orders operated once again till 16.10.2023, when CMA 9983 of 2013 (in Suit 1169 of 2023) was dismissed once again. This application was preferred, along with an urgency application requiring the matter to be listed today, seeking to have the determinant order herein, dated 16.10.2024, suspended on the premise that the appellant may be enabled to file an appeal before the Supreme Court. The memorandum of application and the accompanying affidavit make no mention of any appeal having been filed, despite almost two weeks having passed since this appeal was determined. Irrespective thereof, the appellant has remained a beneficiary of ad interim relief for more than a decade in an application that has concurrently been adjudged to be devoid of merit.

The memorandum of application and the accompanying affidavit make no mention of any appeal having been filed, despite almost two weeks having passed since this appeal was determined. Irrespective thereof, the appellant has remained a beneficiary of ad interim relief for more than a decade in an application that has concurrently been adjudged to be devoid of merit. No case has been set forth to perpetuate an order to which the appellant has been found disentitled, hence, this application is dismissed in limine. Judge Judge Rafiq/PA
